remember: Students will be able to recall the names and locations of early cities in the world such as Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa.
Students will be able to identify the major features of early cities such as cities, towns and villages.
Students will be able to list the tools and technology used in early cities.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of early cities and their significance in the development of human civilization.
Students will be able to explain the social and economic organization of early cities.
Students will be able to describe the relationship between early cities and the surrounding rural areas.
apply: Students will be able to apply the knowledge of early cities to understand the development of later civilizations.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of early cities on the environment and natural resources.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of early cities in the development of trade and commerce.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the similarities and differences between early cities and later civilizations.
Students will be able to compare the social and economic systems of early cities and later civilizations.
Students will be able to evaluate the strengths and weaknesses of early cities in comparison to later civilizations.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To understand the characteristics of early cities and their significance in the development of human civilization.
To explain the major features of Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa and their importance in the history of early cities.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the geographical features of early cities and their impact on the development of human civilization.
To describe the social and economic systems of early cities and their relationship with the surrounding rural areas.
To analyze the role of early cities in the development of trade and commerce.
